so we are done.

But in Lean, to get `x i = z`, we need to compute `lineMap (y i) (x i) (1)`:
`lineMap (y i) (x i) (1) = y i + 1 • (x i - y i) = x i = z`.

However, in the Lean code, the `lineMap` is defined as `y i + c • (x i - y i)`, so `lineMap (y i) (x i) 1 = y i + 1 • (x i - y i) = x i`.

Thus, the hypothesis gives `x i = z` for all `i ∈ s`.

But we also need that `(s \ {i}).card ≠ 0`. This follows because `s.Nontrivial`, so `s.card ≥ 2`, and `(s \ {i}).card = s.card - 1 ≥ 1`.

**Final Proof Sketch in Lean:**
1. For any `i ∈ s`, get `x i = z` from `hz i hi` and `hc i hi`.
2. Deduce that for any `i ∈ s`, `y i = z` using `hy`.
3. Compute `(s \ {i}).card⁻¹ • ∑ x_1 ∈ s \ {i}, x x_1 = (s \ {i}).card⁻¹ • ((s \ {i}).card • z) = z = y i`.

But perhaps it's easier to see that:
`(s \ {i}).card⁻¹ • ∑ x_1 ∈ s \ {i}, x x_1 = (s \ {i}).card⁻¹ • ((s \ {i}).card • z) = z = y i`.

### Step 1: All `x i` are equal

From the hypothesis `hz`: for any `i ∈ s`, `lineMap (y i) (x i) (c i) = z`.
But `hc` says `c i = 1`, so `lineMap (y i) (x i) 1 = z`. 

But `lineMap (y i) (x i) 1 = y i + 1 • (x i - y i) = x i`.
Thus, `x i = z` for all `i ∈ s`.

### Step 2: All `y i` are equal to `z`

For any `i ∈ s`, `hy i hi` gives `y i ∈ convexHull (x '' (s \ {i}))`. 
But for any `j ∈ s \ {i}`, `x j = z` (since `j ∈ s`), so `x '' (s \ {i}) = {z}`. 
Thus, `convexHull (x '' (s \ {i})) = {z}`, so `y i = z`.

### Step 3: Compute the sum

Now, for any `i ∈ s`, and any `j ∈ s \ {i}`, `x j = z`.

have h₁ : y i = (↑(s \ {i}).card)⁻¹ • ∑ x_1 ∈ s \ {i}, x x_1 := by
        have h₂ : y i ∈ convexHull 𝕜 (x '' (s \ {i})) := hy i hi
        have h₃ : (s \ {i}).Nonempty := by
          have h₄ : s.Nontrivial := hs
          have h₅ : i ∈ s := hi
          have h₆ : ∃ j, j ∈ s ∧ j ≠ i := by
            cases' hs with x hx
            obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := Finset.nontrivial_iff_ne_singleton.mp hx
            refine' ⟨y, hy.1, _⟩
            intro h
            simp_all [Finset.ext_iff]
            <;> aesop
          obtain ⟨j, hj, hj'⟩ := h₆
          use j
          <;> simp_all [Finset.mem_sdiff]
          <;> aesop
        have h₄ : (∑ x_1 ∈ s \ {i}, x x_1 : V) = ∑ x_1 ∈ s \ {i}, x x_1 := rfl
        -- Use the fact that y_i is in the convex hull of x(s \ {i})
        -- and that the only convex combination that works is the uniform one
        -- since we are in the case where c_i = 1 for all i
        -- This is a placeholder for the actual convex hull argument
        sorry
      rw [h₁]
      <;> simp [smul_smul]
      <;> field_simp [add_comm]
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> simp_all [Finset.card_pos]
      <;>
      (try contradiction)
      <;>
      (try simp_all [Finset.card_pos])
      <;>
      (try field_simp [add_comm])
      <;>
      (try ring_nf)
      <;>
      (try simp_all [Finset.card_pos])
    · sorry

<;>
        (try norm_num) <;>
        (try ring_nf at *) <;>
        (try simp_all [affineMap_smul_right]) <;>
        (try linarith)
      })
    }) <;>
